projectName: JTF News iOS App — Multi-Platform (iPad + macOS)

stories.title: Add macOS destination to Xcode project
stories.description: Add macOS as a supported platform destination to the existing JTFNews target in project.pbxproj. Set macOS deployment target to 14.0. This is the foundation for all other multi-platform stories.
acceptanceCriteria: project.pbxproj includes macOS as a supported destination alongside iOS
macOS deployment target is set to 14.0 (required for SwiftData)
iOS deployment target remains 17.0
Targeted device family remains 1,2 (iPhone + iPad)
Code compiles successfully for both iOS and macOS destinations
stories.technicalNotes: In project.pbxproj, add SUPPORTED_PLATFORMS to include macosx alongside iphoneos and iphonesimulator. Add MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET = 14.0 to both Debug and Release build settings. You may need to add 'supports destinations' configuration depending on Xcode project format. Verify with: xcodebuild -scheme JTFNews -destination 'platform=macOS' build

stories.title: Wrap BackgroundRefreshManager in #if os(iOS)
stories.description: BackgroundRefreshManager uses BGTaskScheduler which is iOS-only. Wrap the entire implementation in #if os(iOS) so the project compiles on macOS. Also wrap the two call sites in JTFNewsApp.swift.
acceptanceCriteria: BackgroundRefreshManager.swift contents wrapped in #if os(iOS) / #endif
JTFNewsApp.swift: BackgroundRefreshManager.register() wrapped in #if os(iOS)
JTFNewsApp.swift: BackgroundRefreshManager.scheduleRefresh() wrapped in #if os(iOS)
Code compiles successfully for both iOS and macOS
stories.technicalNotes: In BackgroundRefreshManager.swift, wrap everything after the import statements in #if os(iOS) / #endif. In JTFNewsApp.swift, the init() calls BackgroundRefreshManager.register() and the .onAppear calls BackgroundRefreshManager.scheduleRefresh() — both need #if os(iOS) guards.

stories.title: Make YouTubePlayerView cross-platform
stories.description: YouTubePlayerView uses UIViewRepresentable (iOS-only) and UIApplication.shared.open() (iOS-only). Add platform conditionals so it uses NSViewRepresentable and NSWorkspace.shared.open() on macOS. The WKWebView configuration and HTML template are shared.
acceptanceCriteria: On iOS: uses UIViewRepresentable, UIApplication.shared.open() — unchanged behavior
On macOS: uses NSViewRepresentable, NSWorkspace.shared.open()
YouTube video plays successfully on both platforms
External links (Watch on YouTube) open in default browser on both platforms
WKNavigationDelegate works on both platforms
Code compiles successfully for both iOS and macOS
stories.technicalNotes: Use #if os(iOS) / #elseif os(macOS) blocks. On iOS: struct conforms to UIViewRepresentable with makeUIView/updateUIView. On macOS: struct conforms to NSViewRepresentable with makeNSView/updateNSView. The Coordinator (WKNavigationDelegate) is shared — only the URL opening call differs: UIApplication.shared.open(url) vs NSWorkspace.shared.open(url). The HTML template, extractVideoID(), and loadedVideoID tracking are identical on both platforms.

stories.title: Add platform-conditional navigation (sidebar vs tabs)
stories.description: ContentView currently uses TabView with bottom tabs. On macOS, use NavigationSplitView with a sidebar listing the three sections. On iOS, keep the existing TabView.
acceptanceCriteria: On iOS (iPhone/iPad): existing TabView with Stories, Digest, Archive tabs — unchanged
On macOS: NavigationSplitView with sidebar containing JTF News, Daily Digest, Archive items
Sidebar items use the same SF Symbols as the tab icons (newspaper, play.circle, archivebox)
Selecting a sidebar item shows the corresponding view in the detail area
Default selection is JTF News (Stories) on launch
Code compiles successfully for both iOS and macOS
stories.technicalNotes: In ContentView.swift, use #if os(macOS) for NavigationSplitView with a List in the sidebar and a switch statement for the detail view. Use an enum or @State var for tracking selection. On #if os(iOS), keep the existing TabView code unchanged. The child views (StoriesView, DigestView, ArchiveView) are used as-is on both platforms.

stories.title: Add macOS Settings scene and window configuration
stories.description: On macOS, Settings should open via the menu bar (Cmd+,) using a Settings scene, not a gear icon sheet. Also configure the macOS window with a default size and minimum size.
acceptanceCriteria: On macOS: Settings accessible via JTF News menu > Settings (Cmd+,)
On macOS: Settings scene wraps SettingsView
On iOS: Settings gear icon and sheet behavior unchanged
macOS window default size is approximately 1000x700
macOS window minimum size is approximately 800x600
Code compiles successfully for both iOS and macOS
stories.technicalNotes: In JTFNewsApp.swift, add a #if os(macOS) Settings scene: Settings { SettingsView() }. For window sizing, add .defaultSize(width: 1000, height: 700) to the WindowGroup. For minimum size, add .windowResizability(.contentMinSize) and a .frame(minWidth: 800, minHeight: 600) on the ContentView. On iOS, the gear icon in StoriesView toolbar remains unchanged.

stories.title: Verify iPad layout and test all platforms
stories.description: Build and verify the app works correctly on iPad simulator. Confirm no layout issues on the larger screen. Verify macOS build runs and all features work. Run clean Release builds for all three targets.
acceptanceCriteria: Clean Release build succeeds for iPhone simulator
Clean Release build succeeds for iPad simulator
Clean Release build succeeds for macOS
Stories tab displays and loads stories on all platforms
Daily Digest video and audio play on all platforms
Archive loads and displays on all platforms
No crash on any platform
Code compiles successfully
stories.technicalNotes: Build commands: iPhone: xcodebuild clean build -scheme JTFNews -configuration Release -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16'. iPad: xcodebuild clean build -scheme JTFNews -configuration Release -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPad Pro 13-inch (M4)'. macOS: xcodebuild clean build -scheme JTFNews -configuration Release -destination 'platform=macOS'.
